title: Tavuk Sis Chicken Skewers
description: Tavuk Sis is a beloved Turkish classic, featuring succulent chicken pieces marinated in a rich, tangy yogurt and spice blend, then grilled to smoky perfection. Each bite offers a harmonious balance of tender meat, aromatic herbs, and a delightful char, making it a staple in homes and restaurants across Turkey.

ingredients.notes: Grated, for its juice and tenderness
If wooden, soak in water for at least 30 minutes

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the boneless, skinless chicken thighs dry with paper towels. Cut the chicken into uniform 1.5-inch (4cm) cubes. Consistency in size is key for even cooking. Place the chicken pieces into a large mixing bowl.

instructions.title: Make the Authentic Turkish Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, combine the strained yogurt, olive oil, tomato paste, minced garlic, grated onion (squeeze out excess juice if desired, but the juice adds flavor and tenderizes), pul biber, dried oregano, freshly ground black pepper, and salt. Mix thoroughly until all ingredients are well combined into a smooth paste.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Pour the prepared marinade over the chicken cubes in the large mixing bowl. Using your hands, mix well, ensuring every piece of chicken is thoroughly coated with the marinade. Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, or ideally overnight (12-24 hours) for maximum flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Prepare Skewers and Vegetables
instructions.text: If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for at least 30 minutes to prevent burning. If using optional bell peppers and onion, cut them into pieces roughly the same size as the chicken cubes. Thread the marinated chicken onto the skewers, alternating with bell pepper and onion pieces if using. Do not overcrowd the skewers; leave a small space between pieces for even cooking.

instructions.title: Preheat the Grill
instructions.text: Prepare your grill for medium-high heat. For an authentic charcoal grill, aim for glowing embers with no flames. For a gas grill, preheat to medium-high (around 200-230°C / 400-450°F). If using an oven broiler, preheat to high and position the rack about 6-8 inches from the heat source. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Tavuk Sis
instructions.text: Place the skewers on the preheated grill. Cook for 15-20 minutes, turning them every 3-4 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through, tender, and has developed a beautiful char on all sides. The internal temperature of the chicken should reach 74°C (165°F). Be careful not to overcook, as this can dry out the chicken.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Tavuk Sis from the grill and transfer them to a platter. Tent loosely with foil and let them rest for 5 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring tender and flavorful chicken. Serve immediately with traditional Turkish accompaniments like lavash bread, bulgur pilavı, sumac onion salad, grilled tomatoes, and cacık.

tips: For the most authentic flavor, use full-fat strained yogurt (süzme yoğurt) as it provides a richer texture and helps tenderize the chicken without making it watery.
Do not skip the grating of the onion; its juice is a secret ingredient in many Turkish marinades, adding both flavor and incredible tenderness to the meat.
When grilling, avoid poking the chicken too much, as this releases juices. Turn the skewers gently to achieve an even char and cooked exterior.
Always allow the chicken to rest for a few minutes after grilling. This step is crucial for juicy, tender meat.
article:
## History of Tavuk Sis Chicken Skewers

Tavuk Şiş, literally meaning 'chicken skewer,' is a cornerstone of Turkish cuisine, embodying centuries of culinary tradition. Its origins can be traced back to the Ottoman Empire, where various forms of skewered and grilled meats, known as 'kebabs,' were popular. While lamb and beef kebabs often take the spotlight, chicken quickly became a beloved alternative, especially for its tender texture and ability to absorb rich marinades. Tavuk Şiş is more than just a dish; it's a cultural icon found everywhere from bustling street food stalls in Istanbul to elegant fine dining establishments across the country. It represents the Turkish passion for simple, high-quality ingredients transformed by skillful marinating and grilling techniques into something truly extraordinary. The 'şiş' method of cooking over open flames has remained largely unchanged, a testament to its timeless appeal.

## Why This Recipe Works

This Tavuk Sis recipe achieves its authentic flavor and incredible tenderness through a combination of traditional Turkish techniques and ingredients. The strained yogurt in the marinade is paramount; its lactic acid naturally tenderizes the chicken meat, while its fat content keeps the chicken moist and prevents it from drying out during grilling. The addition of grated onion juice further enhances this tenderizing effect and imparts a subtle sweetness. Tomato paste (salça) provides a deep umami base and a rich color, while the blend of pul biber (Aleppo pepper) and dried oregano offers the characteristic earthy and slightly smoky Turkish spice profile. Olive oil ensures the marinade adheres well and contributes to the chicken's succulence. Grilling over medium-high heat creates a beautiful char on the exterior, locking in juices and adding a desirable smoky depth that complements the vibrant marinade, resulting in perfectly cooked, flavorful chicken every time.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

To truly master Tavuk Sis, start with the right cut of chicken: boneless, skinless chicken thighs are highly recommended over breast meat. Thighs have more fat, which translates to juicier, more forgiving results on the grill. Ensure your chicken pieces are cut uniformly to guarantee even cooking; irregular pieces will cook at different rates. The marinating time is non-negotiable – at least 4 hours, but ideally overnight. This allows the yogurt and spices to deeply penetrate and tenderize the meat. When skewering, do not pack the chicken too tightly; leave a small gap between pieces to allow heat to circulate and cook evenly. For grilling, maintain a consistent medium-high heat. Too low, and the chicken won't char; too high, and it will burn before cooking through. Lastly, resist the urge to constantly flip the skewers; let each side develop a good char before turning, usually every 3-4 minutes.

## Serving Suggestions

Authentic Tavuk Sis is best enjoyed with a variety of traditional Turkish accompaniments that balance and enhance its flavors. The most classic pairing is with warm, soft lavash or pide bread, perfect for wrapping the chicken and soaking up its delicious juices. A generous portion of fluffy bulgur pilavı (cracked wheat pilaf) is also a staple, offering a hearty and textural contrast. For a refreshing element, serve with a vibrant shepherd's salad (çoban salatası) featuring finely diced tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and peppers with a lemon-olive oil dressing, or a simple sumac onion salad. A side of cooling cacık, a yogurt-cucumber-garlic dip, is excellent for cutting through the richness. Grilled tomatoes and green peppers, often cooked alongside the skewers, complete the traditional presentation, adding a touch of smoky sweetness and char that perfectly complements the chicken.
